ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 220, PETITIONER NORMAN JUELCH'S PETITION TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF JOYCE JUELCH. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. C.C.P. 2035.010 ALLOWS A PERSON WHO EXPECTS TO BE A PARTY TO AN ACTION TO OBTAIN DISCOVERY FOR THE PURPOSE OF PERPETUATING THE TESTIMONY OF ANOTHER PERSON. PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO HAVE MRS. JUELCH'S DEPOSITION HELD AT OR NEAR HER HOME IN COTTONWOOD, CALIFORNIA IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, MATTER NOT REPORTED.
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-13-2010 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 220

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re: Amended Petition Of Norman Juelch To Perpetuate Testimony Of Joyce Juelch By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for petitioner Norman Juelch

John M. Marston, Esq. of JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for Campbell Soup Company

Brendan J. Tuohy, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant Crane Co.

Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Dana Company

T. Eric Sun, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Emerson Electric Co.

RULING:

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Petitioner Norman Juelch's petition to perpetuate testimony of Joyce Juelch is granted. C.C.P. 2035.010 allows a person who expects to be a party to an action to obtain discovery for the purpose of perpetuating the testimony of another person. Petitioner's request to have Mrs. Juelch's deposition held at or near her home in Cottonwood, California is granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported. =(220/HEK)
